definition: Digital products and services used by employees that enable community relations and connections with the people constituting the environment the organization operates within the and community. The objective is to foster mutual understanding, trust, and support from all parties external to the organization.
notes: From TBM

enrichment.sampleServices: Community event planning; Volunteer program coordination; Donation/sponsorship requests; Community impact reporting
enrichment.offeringConsiderations: Offerings typically vary by audience (internal vs external), channel (press, social, investor, employee comms), approval rigor, and turnaround time. Larger organizations add governance for messaging, brand standards, crisis communications, and regional localization.
enrichment.commercialMarket: Primary market: CSR / corporate giving & volunteering platforms. Scope: Employee giving, matching, volunteering management; grantmaking & sponsorship requests; community impact reporting; event support where needed. Representative vendors: Benevity; Blackbaud YourCause; Bonterra CyberGrants; Goodera; Uncommon Giving; Millie; Submittable (applications); Cvent (events). Typical packaging: Annual SaaS subscription (often quote-based) + implementation; priced by employee count / admins / modules (giving, volunteering, grants) and sometimes transaction/processing fees.

enrichment.offeringConsiderations: Offerings typically vary by audience (internal vs external), channel (press, social, investor, employee comms), approval rigor, and turnaround time. Larger organizations add governance for messaging, brand standards, crisis communications, and regional localization.
enrichment.commercialMarket: Primary market: PR management + media monitoring & press release distribution. definition: Digital products and services used by employees used to communicate with other users, partners or customers.
notes: From TBM

enrichment.sampleServices: Email; Instant messaging; Social communities; Audio conferencing; Video conferencing; Standard telephone; VoIP telephone; Voicemail
enrichment.offeringConsiderations:
Main considerations when defining offerings for this service typically include:
• Urgency and risk: routine communications vs time‑critical executive/corporate‑risk situations (e.g., crisis response with rapid approvals and escalation paths).
• Level of effort: simple templated publishing vs end‑to‑end campaign/program management (planning, content calendar, measurement).
• Audience and geography: whether delivery must be localized by language/culture/time zone, or tailored for specific regions/business units.
enrichment.commercialMarket: Often delivered as a collaboration suite with tiered licenses and add-ons (security/compliance, voice, analytics). Common examples: Microsoft 365 (Teams/Exchange/SharePoint/OneDrive), Google Workspace, Slack, Zoom Workplace.

enrichment.sampleServices: Authentication/Authorization; Identity Management; Identity Governance and Administration; Privileged Access Management; Certificate Management
enrichment.offeringConsiderations:
Identity and access offerings are usually separated based on:
• Access sensitivity: standard workforce access (SSO/MFA and role‑based access) vs privileged access (PAM, elevated approvals, and session recording).
• Population served: employees vs partners/customers (federation, B2B/B2C identity, API access).
• Regulatory constraints: region and data‑residency policy packs are often required to meet local requirements.
• Assurance needs: high‑assurance tiers add stronger authentication, device compliance checks, and deeper audit/reporting.
enrichment.commercialMarket: Typically packaged as Identity & Access Management with tiers for SSO/MFA, conditional access, governance, and privileged access. Examples: Microsoft Entra ID, Okta Workforce Identity, Ping Identity; PAM add-ons like CyberArk or BeyondTrust.
